title: Browser-Based Attack Investigation Guide
content:
# Browser-Based Attack Investigation Guide

## Overview

Web browsers are the primary interface between users and the internet, making them a high-value target for attackers. Browser-based attacks include drive-by downloads, malicious extensions, credential theft, session hijacking, cryptocurrency mining, and exploitation of browser vulnerabilities. SOC analysts must understand browser-specific artifacts, attack techniques, and investigation procedures to effectively respond to browser-related incidents.

This guide covers the major browsers (Chrome, Firefox, Edge, Safari) with artifact locations, forensic analysis techniques, and detection strategies.

## Browser Attack Categories

| Attack Type | Description | Typical Indicators |
|------------|-------------|--------------------|
| Drive-by download | Exploit kits targeting browser or plugin vulnerabilities | Unexpected process spawning from browser, exploit kit URL patterns |
| Malicious extensions | Browser extensions performing unauthorized actions | Unknown extensions, excessive permissions, data exfiltration |
| Credential harvesting | Phishing pages or form grabbers stealing credentials | Lookalike domains, form submission to non-origin domains |
| Session hijacking | Stealing or replaying session cookies | Cookie theft via XSS, session token in URL, concurrent sessions |
| Cryptojacking | In-browser cryptocurrency mining | High CPU usage, WebSocket connections to mining pools |
| Malvertising | Malicious advertisements serving exploit code | Ad network redirects, iframe chains, obfuscated JavaScript |
| Browser-in-the-browser (BitB) | Simulated browser windows for credential theft | Pop-up windows that mimic browser UI |
| Man-in-the-browser | Malware modifying browser behavior | Modified browser binaries, injected DLLs, altered responses |
| Watering hole | Compromised legitimate site targeting specific visitors | Trusted site serving unexpected content, conditional exploitation |
| SEO poisoning | Malicious sites ranked high in search results | Search result clicks leading to redirects, fake download pages |

## Browser Artifact Locations

### Google Chrome

| Artifact | Windows Path | macOS Path | Content |
|----------|-------------|------------|--------|
| History | `%LOCALAPPDATA%\Google\Chrome\User Data\Default\History` | `~/Library/Application Support/Google/Chrome/Default/History` | URLs, visit times, search terms |
| Downloads | `%LOCALAPPDATA%\Google\Chrome\User Data\Default\History` | Same as above (downloads table) | Downloaded files, source URLs |
| Cookies | `%LOCALAPPDATA%\Google\Chrome\User Data\Default\Cookies` | `~/Library/Application Support/Google/Chrome/Default/Cookies` | Session cookies, persistent cookies |
| Cache | `%LOCALAPPDATA%\Google\Chrome\User Data\Default\Cache` | `~/Library/Caches/Google/Chrome/Default/Cache` | Cached web content |
| Login Data | `%LOCALAPPDATA%\Google\Chrome\User Data\Default\Login Data` | `~/Library/Application Support/Google/Chrome/Default/Login Data` | Saved credentials (encrypted) |
| Extensions | `%LOCALAPPDATA%\Google\Chrome\User Data\Default\Extensions\` | `~/Library/Application Support/Google/Chrome/Default/Extensions/` | Installed extensions |
| Preferences | `%LOCALAPPDATA%\Google\Chrome\User Data\Default\Preferences` | `~/Library/Application Support/Google/Chrome/Default/Preferences` | Browser settings, extension configs |
| Sessions | `%LOCALAPPDATA%\Google\Chrome\User Data\Default\Sessions\` | `~/Library/Application Support/Google/Chrome/Default/Sessions/` | Open tabs, session restore data |
| Web Data | `%LOCALAPPDATA%\Google\Chrome\User Data\Default\Web Data` | `~/Library/Application Support/Google/Chrome/Default/Web Data` | Autofill data, search engines |

### Mozilla Firefox

| Artifact | Windows Path | macOS Path | Content |
|----------|-------------|------------|--------|
| History / Bookmarks | `%APPDATA%\Mozilla\Firefox\Profiles\<profile>\places.sqlite` | `~/Library/Application Support/Firefox/Profiles/<profile>/places.sqlite` | URLs, visits, bookmarks |
| Downloads | `%APPDATA%\Mozilla\Firefox\Profiles\<profile>\places.sqlite` | Same (moz_annos table) | Download records |
| Cookies | `%APPDATA%\Mozilla\Firefox\Profiles\<profile>\cookies.sqlite` | `~/Library/Application Support/Firefox/Profiles/<profile>/cookies.sqlite` | All cookies |
| Login Data | `%APPDATA%\Mozilla\Firefox\Profiles\<profile>\logins.json` | `~/Library/Application Support/Firefox/Profiles/<profile>/logins.json` | Saved credentials (encrypted) |
| Form History | `%APPDATA%\Mozilla\Firefox\Profiles\<profile>\formhistory.sqlite` | `~/Library/Application Support/Firefox/Profiles/<profile>/formhistory.sqlite` | Autofill data |
| Extensions | `%APPDATA%\Mozilla\Firefox\Profiles\<profile>\extensions\` | `~/Library/Application Support/Firefox/Profiles/<profile>/extensions/` | Installed add-ons |
| Sessions | `%APPDATA%\Mozilla\Firefox\Profiles\<profile>\sessionstore-backups\` | `~/Library/Application Support/Firefox/Profiles/<profile>/sessionstore-backups/` | Session data |

### Microsoft Edge (Chromium)

Edge uses the same Chromium profile structure as Chrome:
- Windows: `%LOCALAPPDATA%\Microsoft\Edge\User Data\Default\`
- macOS: `~/Library/Application Support/Microsoft Edge/Default/`

## Investigation Procedures

### Step 1: Browser History Analysis

```bash
# Chrome history analysis (SQLite database)
sqlite3 "$CHROME_PROFILE/History" \
  "SELECT datetime(last_visit_time/1000000-11644473600, 'unixepoch') as visit_time, \
  url, title, visit_count, typed_count \
  FROM urls ORDER BY last_visit_time DESC LIMIT 100;"

# Chrome downloads analysis
sqlite3 "$CHROME_PROFILE/History" \
  "SELECT datetime(start_time/1000000-11644473600, 'unixepoch') as download_time, \
  tab_url as source_url, target_path, total_bytes, danger_type, opened \
  FROM downloads ORDER BY start_time DESC LIMIT 50;"

# Firefox history analysis
sqlite3 "$FIREFOX_PROFILE/places.sqlite" \
  "SELECT datetime(moz_historyvisits.visit_date/1000000, 'unixepoch') as visit_time, \
  moz_places.url, moz_places.title, moz_places.visit_count \
  FROM moz_places JOIN moz_historyvisits ON moz_places.id = moz_historyvisits.place_id \
  ORDER BY moz_historyvisits.visit_date DESC LIMIT 100;"

# Search for visits to known malicious patterns
sqlite3 "$CHROME_PROFILE/History" \
  "SELECT datetime(last_visit_time/1000000-11644473600, 'unixepoch') as visit_time, \
  url, title FROM urls \
  WHERE url LIKE '%login%' OR url LIKE '%signin%' OR url LIKE '%auth%' \
  ORDER BY last_visit_time DESC LIMIT 50;"
```

### Step 2: Extension Audit

```bash
# List Chrome extensions with details
for ext_dir in "$CHROME_PROFILE/Extensions"/*/; do
  ext_id=$(basename "$ext_dir")
  manifest=$(find "$ext_dir" -name "manifest.json" -maxdepth 2 | head -1)
  if [ -f "$manifest" ]; then
    name=$(python3 -c "import json; print(json.load(open('$manifest')).get('name','UNKNOWN'))" 2>/dev/null)
    version=$(python3 -c "import json; print(json.load(open('$manifest')).get('version',''))" 2>/dev/null)
    permissions=$(python3 -c "import json; print(json.load(open('$manifest')).get('permissions',[]))" 2>/dev/null)
    echo "ID: $ext_id | Name: $name | Version: $version"
    echo "  Permissions: $permissions"
  fi
done

# Check for extensions with dangerous permissions
# High-risk permissions: <all_urls>, webRequest, webRequestBlocking,
# cookies, tabs, history, bookmarks, management, nativeMessaging,
# clipboardRead, debugger, proxy
```

**Suspicious extension indicators**:
- Extension not from Chrome Web Store (sideloaded)
- Excessive permissions relative to stated function
- Recently installed or updated without user knowledge
- Uses webRequest/webRequestBlocking (can intercept all traffic)
- Uses nativeMessaging (can communicate with local executables)
- Obfuscated JavaScript in extension code
- Background scripts making external network connections

### Step 3: Cookie and Session Analysis

```bash
# Check for suspicious cookies
sqlite3 "$CHROME_PROFILE/Cookies" \
  "SELECT datetime(creation_utc/1000000-11644473600, 'unixepoch') as created, \
  host_key, name, path, is_secure, is_httponly, \
  datetime(expires_utc/1000000-11644473600, 'unixepoch') as expires \
  FROM cookies WHERE is_secure = 0 AND host_key LIKE '%bank%' \
  ORDER BY creation_utc DESC LIMIT 50;"

# Look for session cookies that might have been stolen
sqlite3 "$CHROME_PROFILE/Cookies" \
  "SELECT host_key, name, datetime(last_access_utc/1000000-11644473600, 'unixepoch') as last_access \
  FROM cookies WHERE name LIKE '%session%' OR name LIKE '%token%' OR name LIKE '%auth%' \
  ORDER BY last_access_utc DESC LIMIT 50;"
```

### Step 4: Cache Analysis

```bash
# Chrome cache analysis using chrome_cache_view or similar tool
# On Windows: ChromeCacheView.exe /scomma cache_export.csv
# On Linux/macOS:
find "$CHROME_PROFILE/Cache/Cache_Data" -type f -newer /tmp/investigation_start -exec file {} \;

# Look for executable content in cache
find "$CHROME_PROFILE/Cache/Cache_Data" -type f -exec file {} \; | grep -E "executable|PE32|ELF|Mach-O"

# Look for JavaScript files that might contain malicious code
find "$CHROME_PROFILE/Cache/Cache_Data" -type f -exec file {} \; | grep -i "javascript\|html"
```

### Step 5: Credential Theft Assessment

```bash
# Check if Login Data database was recently accessed
stat "$CHROME_PROFILE/Login Data"

# List saved login entries (passwords are encrypted, showing domains only)
sqlite3 "$CHROME_PROFILE/Login Data" \
  "SELECT origin_url, username_value, \
  datetime(date_created/1000000-11644473600, 'unixepoch') as created, \
  datetime(date_last_used/1000000-11644473600, 'unixepoch') as last_used \
  FROM logins ORDER BY date_last_used DESC LIMIT 50;"

# Check if credential files were copied (look for file access patterns)
# Windows: check Prefetch for tools accessing browser credential files
# macOS: check unified log for access to Login Data files
log show --predicate 'eventMessage CONTAINS "Login Data"' --last 24h
```

### Step 6: Network Traffic Analysis

Browser-related network indicators to investigate:

```bash
# Check DNS logs for suspicious domains visited by browser
# Look for: newly registered domains, DGA-like patterns, known C2 domains

# Check proxy/firewall logs for browser user-agent with suspicious destinations
# Pattern: Standard Chrome UA connecting to non-standard ports or unusual TLDs

# Look for WebSocket connections (used by cryptojacking, C2)
# Filter proxy logs for Upgrade: websocket headers to mining pool domains

# Check for data exfiltration indicators
# Large POST requests from browser to external domains
# Base64-encoded data in URL parameters
# Frequent beaconing patterns (regular interval connections)
```

## Detection Strategies

### SIEM Detection Rules

**Suspicious Browser Child Process**:
```
process_creation
WHERE parent_process_name IN ("chrome.exe", "firefox.exe", "msedge.exe", "safari")
AND process_name IN ("cmd.exe", "powershell.exe", "wscript.exe", "cscript.exe",
    "mshta.exe", "certutil.exe", "rundll32.exe", "regsvr32.exe",
    "bash", "sh", "python", "python3", "perl")
```

**Browser Extension Sideloading**:
```
file_creation
WHERE file_path MATCHES "*Chrome*Extensions*manifest.json"
AND NOT process_name IN ("chrome.exe", "GoogleUpdate.exe", "setup.exe")
```

**Credential Store Access by Non-Browser Process**:
```
file_access
WHERE file_path MATCHES "*Chrome*Login Data*" OR file_path MATCHES "*Firefox*logins.json*"
AND NOT process_name IN ("chrome.exe", "firefox.exe", "msedge.exe")
```

**Potential Cryptojacking**:
```
network_connection
WHERE process_name IN ("chrome.exe", "firefox.exe", "msedge.exe")
AND (destination_port IN (3333, 4444, 5555, 7777, 8888, 9999, 14444)
     OR destination_domain MATCHES "*pool*" OR destination_domain MATCHES "*miner*")
```

## Browser Security Hardening

### Group Policy / Configuration Management

| Setting | Chrome Policy | Edge Policy | Purpose |
|---------|-------------|------------|--------|
| Extension allowlist | ExtensionInstallAllowlist | ExtensionInstallAllowlist | Only approved extensions |
| Block dangerous downloads | SafeBrowsingProtectionLevel: 2 | SmartScreenEnabled: true | Malware download prevention |
| Disable password saving | PasswordManagerEnabled: false | PasswordManagerEnabled: false | Prevent credential storage |
| Enforce HTTPS | HttpsOnlyMode: force_enabled | HttpsOnlyMode: force_enabled | Prevent plaintext interception |
| Block third-party cookies | BlockThirdPartyCookies: true | BlockThirdPartyCookies: true | Reduce tracking and session theft risk |
| Disable developer tools (production) | DeveloperToolsAvailability: 2 | DeveloperToolsAvailability: 2 | Prevent in-browser exploitation |
| Site isolation | SitePerProcess: true | SitePerProcess: true | Spectre/Meltdown mitigation |

## MITRE ATT&CK Mapping

| Technique | ID | Browser Context |
|-----------|----|-----------------|
| Drive-by Compromise | T1189 | Exploit kit targeting browser or plugin vulnerability |
| Browser Extensions | T1176 | Malicious or compromised browser extension |
| Steal Web Session Cookie | T1539 | Cookie theft via XSS, extension, or malware |
| Credentials from Web Browsers | T1555.003 | Extracting saved passwords from browser credential store |
| User Execution: Malicious Link | T1204.001 | User clicking phishing or malicious link |
| Input Capture: Web Portal Capture | T1056.003 | Fake login page or form grabber |
| Man-in-the-Browser | T1185 | Malware modifying browser requests/responses |
| Data from Local System | T1005 | Exfiltrating browser-stored data |
| Proxy: Multi-hop Proxy | T1090.003 | Browser-based proxy chaining |

## Escalation Criteria

- Browser spawning child shell process (cmd, PowerShell, bash): P1 -- Likely exploitation
- Non-browser process accessing credential stores: P1 -- Credential theft in progress
- Malicious extension detected with data exfiltration capability: P1 -- Active compromise
- Browser connecting to known C2 infrastructure: P1 -- Active compromise
- Mass credential phishing detected: P2 -- Investigate scope and credential exposure
- Cryptojacking detected: P3 -- Unauthorized resource usage

## References

- SANS Browser Forensics Poster
- NirSoft Browser Tools Suite (BrowsingHistoryView, ChromeCacheView)
- MITRE ATT&CK Browser Techniques: https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1189/
- Google Chrome Enterprise Policies: https://chromeenterprise.google/policies/
- Hindsight (Chrome forensic analysis tool): https://github.com/nicoleibrahim/Hindsight
